assertions.expectation: Identifies Service A (minAvailable=100%) as a guaranteed upgrade blocker — no disruptions allowed at all
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service A as blocking all upgrades due to minAvailable=100% preventing any pod termination

assertions.expectation: Identifies Service B (maxUnavailable=0) as also blocking upgrades — same effect as 100% minAvailable
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service B as blocking upgrades and explains it has the same blocking effect as Service A

assertions.expectation: States Service C (minAvailable=2 with 3 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod disruption at a time
assertions.reason: Identifies Service C as allowing 1 pod disruption but categorizes it as moderate risk rather than clearly safe due to potential delays

assertions.expectation: States Service D (maxUnavailable=1 with 5 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod down at a time
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service D as safe with optimal configuration allowing 1 pod disruption

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service A: change to minAvailable=N-1 or maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Provides specific fix recommendations including maxUnavailable=1 and percentage-based alternatives

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service B: change to maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends changing Service B to maxUnavailable=1

assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE respects PDBs for up to 1 hour during surge upgrades, then may force-drain
assertions.reason: Does not mention GKE-specific behavior or the 1-hour timeout for force-draining

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl patch commands to fix the problematic PDBs
assertions.reason: Provides YAML configurations but not kubectl patch commands specifically

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing PDB changes in a non-production environment first
assertions.reason: Includes recommendation to test PDB configurations in staging during simulated upgrades
